sections.paragraph:
ἀναιδής, ές (s. ἀναίδεια; Hom. et al.; OGI 665, 16 [adv.]; PLond II, 342, 14 [185 a.d.] p. 174; LXX; Test12Patr) shameless, bold (Jos., Bell. 6, 337; Just., D. 105, 3; Mel., HE 4, 26, 5) ἀ. εἶ you are shameless Hv 3, 3, 2; w. ἰταμός (as Menand., Epitr. 527 S. [351 Kö.) and πολύλαλος m 11:12.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Test12Patr Test12Patr = Testaments of the Twelve Patriarchs, with interpolations II b.c.–III a.d.—List 2

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

Hv Hv = Visions

Menand Menand , various dramas cited; IV–III b.c.—List 5

m m = Mandates

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 64
